Your letters for Saturday, July 2, 2022   Enmax bonuses are shocking I am gobsmacked and disgusted by the bonuses and severance given to various Enmax executives. How can this be? They surely must be embarrassed to announce this at the same time that Enmax is cutting back on customers’ services who can’t pay their whole bill. Alberta budget not as it seems In recent years it has become Alberta government practice to exclude capital expenditures from the budget as if they do not count. I cannot find anything now to suggest that this has changed. Therefore, when $6.7 billion in capital expenditures is taken into account, the government is still spending more than it is taking in. Simple arithmetic tells me the Alberta government is still running a deficit, not the surplus that is being advertised to the public.

Brookman: Canada is an amazing country, but we must ensure it stays that way   Some children start life several yards down the track; some children start life at the starting line and too many others start their lives far back from the starting line. Those children, through no fault of their own, spend a lot of their lives trying to catch up, just trying to reach the group who are already on their way the day they arrive. Many do not ever attain that goal and, as a society, it is up to all of us to try to give those children a boost, not to provide a free ride but simply to provide all that we can to overcome the challenges that they have faced.

Nelson: Globe-hopping Trudeau makes Canadians take to the streets   They see him here; they see him there; the whole world sees him everywhere. Except in the House of Commons, which is where Justin Trudeau belongs.

Opinion: G7 allies can look to Canada as a significant answer to the world's energy problems   As the Group of Seven leaders recently gathered in Germany for their summit meetings, climate change and energy security were on the table for discussion. Given Canada’s unique position as a leading oil and natural gas producer that is taking action to address climate change, Canada’s representatives had every right to be among the loudest voices in the room.

Hole-in-one report: Mickelson National member repeats feat   When Tyson Black is bragging to his buddies about his recent hole-in-one, they might stop him and say, ‘Hey, we’ve heard this story already!’ Not quite. Black carded his first ace last summer on No. 17 at his home club of Mickelson National and just repeated the feat on that signature assignment — a similar shot from a similar spot. As he put it: “Lightning struck twice.” Black followed the exact recipe for his second pin-seeker — a precise seven-iron from 180 yards away.

Chorney-Booth: Pursuit celebrates mountain cuisine with two new restaurants in the Rockies   Banff has perhaps been a bit slower than Calgary in its adoption of an “eat local” philosophy, but over the last few years many chain restaurants on the main drag have given way to more regional fare, and longer-running independent restaurants are moving to create memorable food that evokes a taste of the natural surroundings. Along with the growing juggernaut that is the Banff Hospitality Collective and the collection of restaurants at the Banff Springs Hotel, Pursuit, the global tourism company that grew out of Brewsters, has been leading the charge when it comes to developing a distinctive Rocky Mountain cuisine.

Calgary Horticultural Society: Nasturtiums are a multi-purpose beauty   I fell in love with jewel-coloured nasturtiums after someone dared me to eat one of the flowers. Very dubiously, I picked a lovely, brilliant red one, took a tiny bite and experienced the terrific peppery taste of it. The rest of it followed that first nibble, and I have been munching happily on nasturtium flowers and leaves and pickling their green seeds as capers ever since.

How We Live: The city abounds with people magnets   In our wistfully short summers, I crave being outside. And when the weather gods smile on us, I can’t get enough of walking, cycling, people-watching and dining outdoors in our lovely city.
